system.description:
A small round shield crafted from the overlapping chitin plates of a Rootgnaw Queen — the massive burrowing insect that nests in Yggdrasil's dying root network. The chitin is surprisingly light, warm to the touch, and faintly luminescent with an amber vein-pattern that mirrors the World-Tree's root structure. It smells of turned earth and resin.

1st Level: While you wield this shield, you gain a +6 bonus to Toughness. Additionally, when a creature adjacent to you hits you with a melee attack, the chitin's residual connection to Yggdrasil's root network flares — the attacker takes damage as phantom roots lash at their limbs. This damage increases to against blighted or undead creatures.

5th Level: The shield's bonus to Toughness increases to +12. Additionally, once per encounter, when you are hit by an attack that would reduce you to 0 Toughness, you can use a triggered action to have the chitin absorb the blow — you instead drop to 1 Toughness, and the buckler cracks audibly. Until you take a extended rest, the buckler's reactive damage is disabled, but you gain {resistance +2} as the cracked chitin braces against your arm like a splint.

9th Level: The shield's bonus to Toughness increases to +21. Additionally, the reactive damage increases to (or against blighted/undead), and the once-per-encounter survival ability no longer disables the reactive damage.

Crafting

Prerequisites: Chitin plates from a Rootgnaw Queen, leather strapping, resin sealant from living root-sap

Source: Practical field-craft (no formal texts — improvised from monster remains)

Crafting Time: 450 downtime days

GM Notes

Found: Beat 6 — After the Rootgnaw Brood combat encounter near the Green Heart boundary. The PCs can harvest chitin plates from the Rootgnaw Queen's corpse with a medium Reason test (understanding which plates are structurally sound) or an easy Might test (brute-force extraction). Lew suggests the idea: 'That shell's tougher than anything you'll find at a smith. Might want to keep some.'

Crafting Note: The shield can be assembled during a extended rest by a PC with appropriate skills. If the PCs protected 2+ living roots during the Rootgnaw combat, the chitin retains stronger Yggdrasil resonance — the Director may grant +1 to the reactive damage at 1st level.
